purpose: Maps the self-reinforcing feedback loop connecting AI capex, corporate profits, equity prices, capital flows, and the US dollar to identify systemic vulnerabilities
components: AI capex (~$250B) sustains corporate profits via Kalecki identity
Profits sustain equity prices and S&P 500 earnings growth
Equity prices attract international capital inflows ($40-60B/month)
Capital inflows support dollar strength (AI premium 5-8 cents EUR/USD)
Dollar strength supports margins for US multinationals
Margins sustain capex capacity (OCF/capex 1.5-1.7x) and loop restarts
Fiscal deficit floor ($1.8-2T) provides partial insulation against collapse
current_assessment: Loop currently in strengthening mode with all linkages active. Fiscal floor means 25% AI capex cut is only 3-4% of fiscal floor, limiting Kalecki-driven earnings collapse. Key vulnerability is international capital flow persistence.
limitations: AI capex is marginal not dominant driver vs fiscal deficit (10-15x larger)
Dollar-equity beta is qualitative; linear extrapolation was refuted
Loop reversal dynamics are theoretically sound but empirically untested at this scale
Fiscal consolidation could paradoxically weaken the loop through Kalecki channel
